Tree Maintenance Assignments

  • Nearest Person
  • Balanced Nearest
  • Minimum Distance
  • Rebalanced
  • Weighted Zones

Each tree is assigned to the closest volunteer. This is the simplest approach and keeps travel distances short, but some volunteers may end up with many more trees than others.

Total — — 50.2

Trees are assigned to the closest volunteer who still has room. Volunteers near more trees fill up first, then nearby trees go to the next-closest volunteer. This spreads the workload more evenly while still keeping assignments fairly close to each volunteer.

Assignments are chosen to minimize the total travel distance across all volunteers while keeping everyone’s workload roughly equal. This finds the best overall balance between short trips and fair distribution.

Total — — 50.2

Starts with the nearest-person assignment, then moves trees between neighboring volunteers to even out the workload. Only trees near the boundary between two volunteers are moved, so the assignments stay geographically sensible.

of trees		 Total
miles

Total — — 54.1

Adjusts the size of each volunteer’s territory until everyone has roughly the same number of trees. Volunteers with too few trees get a larger zone; volunteers with too many get a smaller one. This keeps each person’s trees in a single contiguous area.
